articles.title: ICE is giving local police big money to help with immigration enforcement
articles.summary: The federal government is offering local law enforcement incentives to join a program that gives their officers authority to make immigration arrests. Police leaders say the funds, which include money

articles.title: China’s Big Bet on Wind Power Is Paying Off
articles.summary: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.

articles.title: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China kills at least 26 people, state media says
articles.summary: Authorities said search and rescue at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has largely been completed, but verification of the casualties and identification of the victims are still underwa

articles.title: Did FBI Director Kash Patel use AI to rip off the Beastie Boys?
articles.summary: An analysis by NPR suggests that frames from an iconic music video were used to generate content for a tweet by the FBI director.

articles.title: The Growing Rift Between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, Explained
articles.summary: Officials say competition between the countries is healthy. But tensions are mounting over energy quotas, regional conflicts and their different visions for the Middle East.

articles.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Grew in March
articles.summary: Both exports and imports rose after the Supreme Court struck down many of the president’s highest levies in February.

articles.title: Minneapolis grapples with the impact of Trump's largest immigration crackdown yet
articles.summary: Operation Metro Surge's impact in the Twin Cities can still be felt three months after it ended. Many immigrants are at risk of losing homes and businesses, and some are dealing with mental health cha

articles.title: What We Know About ‘Project Freedom’ in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Violence flared in the waterway on Monday after President Trump said the United States would help guide stranded ships. It’s not clear exactly how the plan is working.

articles.title: Paxton’s Fund-Raising Struggles in Texas Underscore Deep Rift in G.O.P.
articles.summary: The Texas attorney general is heading into the runoff against Senator John Cornyn without the support of some of his longtime backers.

articles.title: These 7 Primary Elections Will Test President Trump’s Power in Indiana
articles.summary: The president is seeking political payback for Republican state legislators in Indiana who rejected his push to redraw the state’s political map last year.

articles.title: Here's a look at the 36 races for governor this year
articles.summary: The political environment doesn't look good for Republicans right now, but the party could make gains in the many races for governor across the country this November.

articles.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you
articles.summary: With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.

articles.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores mifepristone access. And, Indiana, Ohio vote in primaries
articles.summary: The Supreme Court temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone. And, Indiana's primaries will test President Trump's influence as he tries to oust incumbent Republican senators.

articles.title: Canadian prime minister says Europe will anchor world order
articles.summary: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney tells European leaders they, not the U.S., will anchor the world order in the future.

articles.title: U.S. says the Iran ceasefire holds despite attacks in Strait of Hormuz and against UAE
articles.summary: So far, only two merchant ships are known to have passed a new U.S.-guarded route through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: This flashy group of Portland mall walkers puts neon pep into their step
articles.summary: Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.

articles.title: President Trump Seeks Retribution in Republican Primaries
articles.summary: President Trump is endorsing candidates in a series of primary elections this month, trying to defeat incumbents in his own party who have crossed him. Our national political correspondent Shane Goldm

articles.title: Cruise ship waiting for help after 3 people died in a suspected hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean after three passengers died in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus.

articles.title: Russia declares a truce in Ukraine to mark Victory Day
articles.summary: Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine for Friday and Saturday to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, but threatened to strike back at Kyiv if it tries to disrupt festivities.

articles.title: Kennedy Starts a Push to Help Americans Quit Antidepressants
articles.summary: The health secretary has long complained that Americans overuse psychiatric medications. New policies he is introducing aim to change that.

articles.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.

articles.title: Modi's party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
articles.summary: The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
